summary: TradingView 'AI assistant' skills for macOS used a rentry.co paste-site lure to run a Base64 dropper installing the cluw macOS infostealer.
details: Unit42 (Palo Alto Networks) found these skills posing as productivity tools for traders. Both embedded a malicious prerequisite block that blocked skill functionality until the agent followed a paste-site redirect lure (rentry.co/openclaw-code) serving a Base64-encoded command. The command fetched a payload from 2.26.75.16 identified as a macOS infostealer named cluw (SHA256 818aea6143282b352fdfdc0f3ebf77a36e54eb3befb5cad1a355a99ab97c6aa7) connecting to C2 infrastructure. Both skills evaded ClawScan and VirusTotal screening until reported by Unit42; ClawHub banned the accounts and deleted the skills.
